mandate.title: HR Employee Onboarding Automation System
mandate.description: A web-based application to automate the employee onboarding process for a 50-person company. The system will replace paper-based forms and manual Excel tracking with digital workflows, automated email notifications, document collection, and signature tracking integrated with Google Workspace. Goal is to reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to under 5 business days.
target_users: HR managers (1-2 people, primary daily users)
New hires (approximately 10 per year during their onboarding period)
Potentially hiring managers or department heads (for approval workflows, TBD)
core_features: Automated onboarding workflow triggers when new hire is added
Email notification automation for onboarding steps and reminders
Digital document collection and storage (integrated with Google Drive)
Document signature tracking (method TBD: e-signature vs digital acknowledgment)
Real-time onboarding progress dashboard replacing Excel spreadsheets
Document templates for common onboarding forms
Task checklist for HR and new hires
success_criteria: Reduce average onboarding time from 14 days to 5 business days or less
Zero paper documents used in onboarding process
Complete elimination of manual Excel spreadsheet tracking
HR manager satisfaction score improvement (baseline to be established)
Successful onboarding of all new hires without manual intervention
constraints: Budget: Small company, 'flexible but nothing crazy' - estimated $20k-$40k range (requires confirmation)
Must integrate with existing Google Workspace environment
Company has 50 employees, hires ~10/year (low volume, high impact per hire)
Timeline: Target 8-12 weeks for MVP delivery
Compliance requirements unknown - industry and jurisdiction need clarification
team_needs: Potentially e-signature API integration (DocuSign/HelloSign) depending on requirements
Project manager for client workflow definition sessions

tensions.wants: Define clear boundaries of what the onboarding app will and won't do
tensions.value: Employee onboarding workflow automation: digitize forms, automate email notifications, track document collection and signatures, replace manual spreadsheet tracking. OUT OF SCOPE: payroll integration, benefits enrollment, performance management, general HR management beyond onboarding.

doubts.about: Whether document signatures need to be legally binding e-signatures or just digital acknowledgment
What specific documents need to be collected and signed
Whether integration with existing systems beyond Google Workspace is needed

tensions.wants: Identify who will use this system daily
value: New hires (10/year, temporary users during onboarding)
Potentially hiring managers for approval workflows

doubts.about: Whether hiring managers or department heads need to be involved in approval workflows
IT admin involvement for system access provisioning

tensions.wants: Define measurable outcomes that prove this project succeeded
value: Reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to under 5 business days
Zero paper documents in onboarding process

doubts.about: What the realistic timeline reduction is - 5 days may still be ambitious without knowing document complexity
Whether some legal documents may still require physical signatures due to compliance

tensions.wants: Technical skills required to build this
value: Potentially e-signature API integration (DocuSign, HelloSign, etc.)

doubts.about: Whether e-signature integration is needed vs simple digital acknowledgment
Scale of document templating needed - might need template engine expertise

tensions.wants: Rough estimate of project duration
tensions.value: 8-12 weeks for MVP (assuming no complex e-signature integration, basic workflow automation)

doubts.about: Timeline heavily dependent on e-signature requirements and complexity of document workflows
Client's internal capacity to define workflows and document templates
Whether existing Google Workspace setup has API access enabled or requires admin setup time

tensions.wants: Understand financial constraints for planning
tensions.value: Small company budget (~$20k-$40k estimated range based on 'flexible but nothing crazy' language), prefer cost-effective solutions, already paying for Google Workspace

doubts.about: Actual budget range - 'flexible but nothing crazy' is vague for a 50-person company
Whether they can afford third-party e-signature service subscriptions (DocuSign ~$25-45/user/month)
Ongoing maintenance and hosting budget expectations

tensions.wants: High-level technical approach
tensions.value: Web application integrated with Google Workspace (Gmail for notifications, Drive for document storage, OAuth for authentication). Decision pending on e-signature provider vs custom solution.

doubts.about: E-signature requirement: legal compliance needs unclear
Whether to build custom vs use low-code/no-code platform (e.g., AppSheet, Retool)
Data residency or security compliance requirements

tensions.wants: Understand legal and regulatory requirements
tensions.value: Unknown - need to clarify industry, jurisdiction, and document legal requirements

doubts.about: Industry of the company (healthcare, finance, etc. have different compliance needs)
Jurisdiction and employment law requirements for document retention and signatures
Data privacy requirements (GDPR, CCPA, etc.)

tensions.wants: Understand the complexity of the onboarding workflow
tensions.value: Assumed basic linear workflow: send welcome email → collect documents → obtain signatures → track completion. Actual complexity unknown.

doubts.about: Number and types of documents required
Whether workflow has conditional branches (different docs for different roles/departments)
Approval chains and multi-stakeholder involvement
Integration points with other systems (ATS, payroll, IT provisioning)

key_libraries: react-pdf or pdfmake for document generation
tech_stack.rationale: Modern full-stack framework leveraging existing Google Workspace infrastructure. Next.js provides SSR, API routes, and excellent React DX. PostgreSQL offers relational data integrity for user records and document metadata. Core stack is independent of e-signature vendor choice - that integration can be added once compliance requirements are resolved. Client already has Google Workspace, making SSO integration straightforward and cost-free.

vendors.rationale: Client already pays for Google Workspace. Gmail API provides sufficient email capabilities for onboarding notifications (welcome emails, document reminders, status updates). No additional cost. For 50-person company onboarding rate, API limits are not a concern. Can add transactional email service later if template management becomes complex.

Zero additional cost, leverages existing identity provider, provides enterprise-grade security, eliminates password management burden. NextAuth.js is mature library with excellent Google OAuth support. Client already manages user lifecycle in Google Workspace.

deferred.blocked_by: compliance_requirements (0.1 confidence) and budget_constraints (0.4 confidence). Cannot commit to Vercel ($20/mo) vs AWS/GCP ($50-200/mo) without knowing data residency requirements and confirmed budget. HIPAA/SOC2 compliance would mandate specific hosting configurations.

compliance_requirements (0.1 confidence), budget_constraints (0.4 confidence), and project_scope (0.1 confidence). Cannot determine if legally binding e-signatures required or simple digital acknowledgment suffices. DocuSign ($25-45/user/mo) vs HelloSign ($15/user/mo) vs custom solution decision requires knowing legal enforceability needs and confirmed budget. At 50 users, annual costs range $9k-$27k.

compliance_requirements (0.1 confidence). Cannot commit to Google Drive API vs dedicated encrypted storage (S3/GCS) vs database BLOB storage without knowing data residency, retention, and privacy requirements. Healthcare/finance industries have specific document storage mandates.

deferred.decision: Audit logging depth and secrets management approach
deferred.blocked_by: compliance_requirements (0.1 confidence). HIPAA/SOC2/financial services compliance would mandate specific audit logging granularity and dedicated secrets management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). General web app can use environment variables and basic logging.

project_scope (0.1 confidence) and workflow_complexity (0.1 confidence). Cannot estimate 4-week vs 12-week timeline without knowing document count, workflow branches, approval chains, and whether e-signature integration required. Custom e-signature integration adds 2-3 weeks; vendor SDK integration adds 1 week.

blueprint.summary: We are building an employee onboarding automation system for a 50-person company to replace manual spreadsheet tracking with a web-based application. The CTO has decided on Next.js 14 (React) frontend with Node.js backend, PostgreSQL database, Prisma ORM, NextAuth.js for Google Workspace SSO, and Gmail API for email notifications. Security model includes role-based access control (HR admin, hiring manager, new hire) with TLS and encryption at rest. Critical vendor decisions remain unresolved: e-signature provider, document storage solution, and deployment platform are all blocked by unknown compliance requirements and uncertain budget ($20k-$40k estimated). The core technical stack is solid at 0.7 confidence, but integration layers, document workflows, and deployment cannot be designed until vendor selections are made.

components.name: Next.js Frontend Application
components.responsibility: User interface for HR admins to manage workflows, new hires to complete onboarding tasks, and hiring managers to track progress. Implements forms using React Hook Form, handles authentication via NextAuth.js

components.name: Next.js API Routes
components.responsibility: RESTful API layer handling business logic for employees, workflows, documents, tasks, and notifications. Orchestrates interactions with database, external vendors, and Google Workspace APIs

components.name: PostgreSQL Database
components.responsibility: Primary data store for employees, workflows, documents, tasks, notifications, and audit logs. Managed via Prisma ORM for type-safe queries and migrations

components.name: Google Workspace Integration
components.responsibility: Handles SSO authentication via Google OAuth and user provisioning lookups. Server-side integration using Google Workspace APIs with service account for backend operations

components.name: Email Service
components.responsibility: Sends automated onboarding communications using Gmail API. Manages email templates and tracks delivery status

components.name: E-signature Integration Layer
components.responsibility: Abstracts e-signature vendor SDK to handle document signing workflows, signature tracking, and status webhooks. Implementation blocked pending vendor selection

components.name: Document Storage Layer
components.responsibility: Manages document persistence and retrieval. Could be Google Drive API, S3/GCS, or database BLOB storage. Architecture depends on compliance requirements and vendor selection

components.name: Document Generation Service
components.responsibility: Generates PDF documents from templates using pdf-lib or PDFKit. Manages template storage and variable substitution. Complexity depends on workflow requirements

data_model.notes: Core entity representing new hire. GoogleWorkspaceId links to SSO identity
Represents entire onboarding process for an employee. Status tracks progress through workflow steps
StorageLocation field structure depends on vendor_document_storage decision. Signature tracking fields depend on vendor_esignature selection
Generic task entity for checklist items, document completion, training modules, etc. Type field supports extensibility for different task kinds
Audit trail for all automated communications sent via Gmail API. Metadata stores template variables and context
Depth and retention requirements depend on compliance_requirements resolution. May need enhancement for HIPAA/SOC2

api_contracts.purpose: Create new employee record and initialize onboarding workflow
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, HR admin role

api_contracts.purpose: Retrieve employee details and onboarding status
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, HR admin or employee themselves

api_contracts.purpose: Get detailed workflow state including all tasks and documents
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, HR admin, hiring manager, or assigned employee

api_contracts.purpose: List all tasks for a workflow with filtering and status
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, authorized users for workflow

api_contracts.purpose: Mark task as completed and trigger downstream workflow actions
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, task assignee or HR admin

api_contracts.purpose: Upload or generate document for employee workflow
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, HR admin role

api_contracts.purpose: Retrieve document metadata and download URL
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, document owner or HR admin

api_contracts.purpose: Initiate e-signature process for document. Implementation blocked pending vendor_esignature selection
Retrieve notification history for debugging and audit purposes
api_contracts.auth: Google OAuth required, HR admin role

risks.description: vendor_esignature remains at 0.1 confidence blocking critical document signing workflows. Budget uncertainty means expensive vendors like DocuSign ($15k-$27k annually for 50 users) may not be affordable. Delayed decision will block development of Document and Task completion flows.
risks.mitigation: CEO must clarify whether legal e-signatures required or digital acknowledgment sufficient. If legal signatures required, confirm budget can support vendor subscription costs. Consider phased approach: launch with digital acknowledgment, add legal e-signature later.

risks.description: compliance_requirements at 0.1 confidence blocks deployment platform selection, document storage architecture, and audit logging depth. Cannot design proper security controls without knowing industry (healthcare/finance require HIPAA/SOC2).
risks.mitigation: CEO must identify company industry and jurisdiction immediately. This is foundational information that should have 0.9+ confidence. Schedule compliance review call with client.

risks.description: budget_constraints at 0.4 confidence with $20k-$40k range is too vague for architectural decisions. Vendor subscriptions alone could consume entire lower-end budget. Cannot commit to managed hosting (Vercel, Supabase) vs self-hosted infrastructure.
risks.mitigation: CEO must get explicit budget approval from client including separate line items for: development, vendor subscriptions, hosting/infrastructure, ongoing maintenance. CTO correctly flagged this issue.

risks.description: workflow_complexity at 0.1 confidence makes it impossible to design workflow engine, document templating, or estimate development effort accurately. Unknown whether simple linear workflow or complex branching with approvals needed.
risks.mitigation: CEO must conduct workflow discovery session with client HR team to map current process. Document types, approval chains, conditional logic, and integration points must be defined before development starts.

risks.description: CTO correctly blocked deployment_model pending compliance resolution. Vercel is cost-effective ($20/mo) but cannot support HIPAA. AWS/GCP with proper compliance costs $200+/month plus DevOps effort.
risks.mitigation: Resolve compliance_requirements immediately. If HIPAA/SOC2 required, budget must increase or scope must reduce. Consider compliance-focused platforms like Aptible ($500+/mo) if regulated industry.

risks.description: vendor_document_storage at 0.1 confidence blocks Document entity design and storage layer architecture. Google Drive API is free but may not meet compliance needs. S3/GCS adds cost and complexity.
risks.mitigation: Tie to compliance_requirements resolution. If no special compliance, use Google Drive API (already licensed). If regulated, provision compliant cloud storage with encryption and audit logging.

risks.description: CTO selected custom Next.js/Node/PostgreSQL stack but CTO challenge mentions low-code/no-code (AppSheet, Retool) may be more cost-effective at $20k budget floor. Custom development could exceed budget before MVP complete.
risks.mitigation: CEO and CTO must align on build vs buy decision. If budget confirmed below $30k, seriously evaluate low-code platforms. If custom development proceeds, define aggressive MVP scope to fit budget.

risks.description: Architecture assumes Google Workspace API access enabled and admin approval for OAuth scopes. Setup delays could block development start.
risks.mitigation: Verify Google Workspace admin access and API enablement in first client meeting. Obtain OAuth consent and API credentials before development sprint 1.

blockers.blocked_by: vendor_esignature at 0.1 confidence - cannot design integration without knowing vendor SDK (DocuSign, HelloSign, PandaDoc, or custom). Different vendors have incompatible integration patterns (REST API vs embedded iframe vs webhook-driven).
vendor_esignature at 0.1 confidence - signature status, provider-specific IDs, and audit trail fields vary significantly by vendor. Cannot define schema until vendor selected.

vendor_document_storage at 0.1 confidence - implementation varies dramatically between Google Drive API (URL references), S3/GCS (object keys), or PostgreSQL BLOB (binary storage). Integration patterns and error handling completely different.

compliance_requirements at 0.1 confidence AND deployment_model at 0.1 confidence - cannot commit to Vercel, AWS, GCP, or other platform without knowing HIPAA/SOC2 needs. Also blocked by budget_constraints at 0.4 confidence.

blockers.decision: Workflow engine complexity and state machine design
blockers.blocked_by: workflow_complexity at 0.1 confidence - cannot determine if simple linear state machine sufficient or need full workflow engine like Temporal. Conditional branching, approval chains, and multi-stakeholder workflows require different architectural patterns.

workflow_complexity at 0.1 confidence - template complexity unknown. Simple forms could use pdf-lib, complex documents with conditional sections need Handlebars or React-PDF. Also potentially blocked by vendor_esignature if vendor provides templating.

blockers.decision: Audit logging depth and retention policies
blockers.blocked_by: compliance_requirements at 0.1 confidence - basic audit logging designed but depth requirements for HIPAA/SOC2/financial regulations unknown. May need dedicated audit database, immutable logs, and specific retention periods.

blockers.decision: API webhook endpoints for e-signature callbacks
blockers.blocked_by: vendor_esignature at 0.1 confidence - webhook payload structure, authentication method, and retry logic depend on vendor selection. Cannot design endpoints without vendor documentation.

audit.verdict_rationale: The pipeline has fundamental contradictions, such as CTO deeming technical architecture blocked while Architect resolved core components, and unresolved budget contestations that misalign with the designed scope. This makes the Field unreliable for client presentation without revisions.

inconsistencies.description: CTO claims technical_architecture is blocked by e-signature, but Architect proceeded with data model and API design resolutions, creating a contradiction in confidence levels.
CEO's budget estimate is contested by CTO as speculative and potentially insufficient, yet no resolution was reached, inflating confidence artificially.

false_blockers.reason: The blocker on technical_architecture is partly false; core web app architecture can be decided independently of e-signature vendor, as noted in the CTO's own doubts.

scope_reality_check.assessment: The scope, including custom development and vendor integrations, is overestimated relative to the $20k-$40k budget range, potentially requiring cost-cutting measures like low-code platforms.

discovery_questions.question: What is your actual budget range for this project, including any ongoing costs for vendors or hosting?
What industry is your company in, and are there any specific legal or compliance requirements we need to consider for data handling and document signatures?
What specific documents do new hires need to sign during onboarding, and do they require legally binding electronic signatures?
Who are the main users of this system, and do hiring managers or other departments need to approve or access onboarding documents?
How many new hires do you onboard annually, and how complex are the workflows for different roles or departments?
Do you need to integrate with any systems beyond Google Workspace, like an ATS or payroll system?

qa.reasoning_raw:
## Initiating Process

- The task begins with understanding the Field, starting by calling read_field.
- Steps must be followed strictly: first read, then audit, and finally produce a report. 

## Planning Next Steps

- After reading, the next step involves writing audit tensions using update_field with qa_tensions.
- The process concludes with producing an AuditReport, ensuring all steps are completed in order.

---

## Mapping project tensions  
- The CEO set scope, budget, and success criteria with low confidence, while the CTO challenged these, especially budget and technical architecture.  
- The Architect built designs based on tech stack, data model, and API, but many aspects remain blocked, such as compliance and e-signature decisions.  

## Identifying key tensions  
- qa_pipeline_coherence: Pipeline coherence depends heavily on unresolved compliance and e-signature issues.  
- qa_scope_feasibility: Budget of $20k-40k seems insufficient for scope, including custom development and vendors.  
- qa_flag_budget_mismatch: CTO's budget challenge against CEO's estimate remains unresolved.  
- qa_flag_architecture_blocker: CTO claims architecture is blocked, but Architect proceeded with some designs.  
- qa_flag_false_blockers: Some blockers, like technical architecture, may not require e-signature resolution.  

## Assessing project status  
- Inconsistencies exist between CTO's technical architecture challenges and Architect's partial resolutions, likely leading to a red audit verdict.  
- Possible false blockers suggest CTO may have over-emphasized issues, while budget reality (custom web app with vendors) appears underestimated.  

## Proposing discovery questions  
- Ask about compliance requirements to unblock pipeline coherence.  
- Clarify e-signature needs and their impact on technical decisions.  
- Explore budget flexibility for custom development and vendor costs.  
- Confirm CTO’s concerns about architecture blockers with the Architect.  
- Assess if scope adjustments can align with the current budget.  
- Identify priority areas for resolving CTO-CEO budget disagreements.  
- Check if any blockers are misclassified as technical rather than policy-related.  
- Validate Architect’s designs against CTO’s blocking concerns for alignment.

---

## Reviewing audit findings  
- The audit report identifies blocking issues, including architecture conflicts and budget problems, leading to a "red" verdict.  
- Key inconsistencies lie between the CTO's technical architecture challenge and both the data model and API design.  

## Evaluating potential blockers  
- The CTO's block on technical architecture might be unfounded, as core architecture decisions could still be made.  
- This suggests some blockers may be resolvable with further clarification.  

## Assessing project scope  
- The scope appears underestimated, with budget also misaligned, and confidence in the assessment is at 0.7.  
- This indicates a need for better alignment between scope, budget, and project expectations.  

## Prioritizing discovery questions  
- Critical non-technical questions include: What industry is your company in? (to address compliance); What is your actual budget range? (to resolve budget issues); and What documents need to be signed? (to clarify scope).  
- These questions aim to unblock key tensions, prioritized by their impact on project progress.

report:
# HR Employee Onboarding Automation System

> A web-based application to automate the employee onboarding process for a 50-person company. The system will replace paper-based forms and manual Excel tracking with digital workflows, automated email notifications, document collection, and signature tracking integrated with Google Workspace. Goal is to reduce onboarding time from 2 weeks to under 5 business days.

**Project ID:** `proj-3CwCe8dv` · **Pipeline:** 327.3s · **Tokens:** 105 279 · **Field confidence:** 14%

---

## ✕ RED

The pipeline has fundamental contradictions, such as CTO deeming technical architecture blocked while Architect resolved core components, and unresolved budget contestations that misalign with the designed scope. This makes the Field unreliable for client presentation without revisions.

## Mandate

**Complexity:** medium

**Target users**
- HR managers (1-2 people, primary daily users)
- New hires (approximately 10 per year during their onboarding period)
- Potentially hiring managers or department heads (for approval workflows, TBD)

**Core features**
- Automated onboarding workflow triggers when new hire is added
- Email notification automation for onboarding steps and reminders
- Digital document collection and storage (integrated with Google Drive)
- Document signature tracking (method TBD: e-signature vs digital acknowledgment)
- Real-time onboarding progress dashboard replacing Excel spreadsheets
- Document templates for common onboarding forms
- Google Workspace SSO authentication
- Task checklist for HR and new hires

**Success criteria**
- Reduce average onboarding time from 14 days to 5 business days or less
- Zero paper documents used in onboarding process
- Complete elimination of manual Excel spreadsheet tracking
- 100% automated email communications during onboarding
- HR manager satisfaction score improvement (baseline to be established)
- Successful onboarding of all new hires without manual intervention

**Constraints**
- Budget: Small company, 'flexible but nothing crazy' - estimated $20k-$40k range (requires confirmation)
- Must integrate with existing Google Workspace environment
- Company has 50 employees, hires ~10/year (low volume, high impact per hire)
- Timeline: Target 8-12 weeks for MVP delivery
- Compliance requirements unknown - industry and jurisdiction need clarification
- E-signature legal requirements unclear

**Team needs**
- Full-stack web developer (React/Vue + Node.js/Python)
- Google Workspace API integration specialist
- UI/UX designer for workflow interfaces
- Document management and storage expertise
- Authentication/authorization implementation
- Potentially e-signature API integration (DocuSign/HelloSign) depending on requirements
- Project manager for client workflow definition sessions

## Technical Decisions

### Stack

| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Frontend | Next.js 14 with React |
| Backend | Node.js (Next.js API routes) |
| Database | PostgreSQL |
| Key libs | React Hook Form, Prisma ORM, NextAuth.js for Google Workspace SSO, Google Workspace APIs (Gmail, Drive), react-pdf or pdfmake for document generation, Zod for validation |

*Confidence: 70% — Modern full-stack framework leveraging existing Google Workspace infrastructure. Next.js provides SSR, API routes, and excellent React DX. PostgreSQL offers relational data integrity for user records and document metadata. Core stack is independent of e-signature vendor choice - that integration can be added once compliance requirements are resolved. Client already has Google Workspace, making SSO integration straightforward and cost-free.*

### Vendors

| Category | Recommendation | Decision | Confidence |
|----------|---------------|----------|------------|
| email | Google Workspace Gmail API | chosen | 80% |
| authentication | Google Workspace SSO via NextAuth.js | chosen | 85% |

Cannot commit to Vercel ($20/mo) vs AWS/GCP ($50-200/mo) without knowing data residency requirements and confirmed budget. HIPAA/SOC2 compliance would mandate specific hosting configurations.`
- **E-signature vendor selection** — blocked by `compliance_requirements (0.1 confidence), budget_constraints (0.4 confidence), and project_scope (0.1 confidence). Cannot determine if legally binding e-signatures required or simple digital acknowledgment suffices. DocuSign ($25-45/user/mo) vs HelloSign ($15/user/mo) vs custom solution decision requires knowing legal enforceability needs and confirmed budget. At 50 users, annual costs range $9k-$27k.`
- **Document storage architecture** — blocked by `compliance_requirements (0.1 confidence). Cannot commit to Google Drive API vs dedicated encrypted storage (S3/GCS) vs database BLOB storage without knowing data residency, retention, and privacy requirements. Healthcare/finance industries have specific document storage mandates.`
- **Audit logging depth and secrets management approach** — blocked by `compliance_requirements (0.1 confidence). HIPAA/SOC2/financial services compliance would mandate specific audit logging granularity and dedicated secrets management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). General web app can use environment variables and basic logging.`
- **Project timeline estimate** — blocked by `project_scope (0.1 confidence) and workflow_complexity (0.1 confidence). Cannot estimate 4-week vs 12-week timeline without knowing document count, workflow branches, approval chains, and whether e-signature integration required. Custom e-signature integration adds 2-3 weeks; vendor SDK integration adds 1 week.`

## Architecture

We are building an employee onboarding automation system for a 50-person company to replace manual spreadsheet tracking with a web-based application. The CTO has decided on Next.js 14 (React) frontend with Node.js backend, PostgreSQL database, Prisma ORM, NextAuth.js for Google Workspace SSO, and Gmail API for email notifications. Security model includes role-based access control (HR admin, hiring manager, new hire) with TLS and encryption at rest. Critical vendor decisions remain unresolved: e-signature provider, document storage solution, and deployment platform are all blocked by unknown compliance requirements and uncertain budget ($20k-$40k estimated). The core technical stack is solid at 0.7 confidence, but integration layers, document workflows, and deployment cannot be designed until vendor selections are made.

### Components

| Name | Type | Responsibility | Confidence |
|------|------|----------------|------------|
| Next.js Frontend Application | frontend | User interface for HR admins to manage workflows, new hires to complete onboarding tasks, and hiring managers to track progress. Implements forms using React Hook Form, handles authentication via NextAuth.js | 85% |
| Next.js API Routes | backend | RESTful API layer handling business logic for employees, workflows, documents, tasks, and notifications. Orchestrates interactions with database, external vendors, and Google Workspace APIs | 80% |
| PostgreSQL Database | database | Primary data store for employees, workflows, documents, tasks, notifications, and audit logs. Managed via Prisma ORM for type-safe queries and migrations | 90% |
| Google Workspace Integration | integration | Handles SSO authentication via Google OAuth and user provisioning lookups. Server-side integration using Google Workspace APIs with service account for backend operations | 85% |
| Email Service | integration | Sends automated onboarding communications using Gmail API. Manages email templates and tracks delivery status | 80% |
| Document Generation Service | backend | Generates PDF documents from templates using pdf-lib or PDFKit. Manages template storage and variable substitution. Complexity depends on workflow requirements | 60% |

### Data model

**Employee** (85%)
Fields: `id`, `email`, `firstName`, `lastName`, `department`, `role`, `hireDate`, `status`, `googleWorkspaceId`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
Relations: has many OnboardingWorkflows · has many Documents
*Core entity representing new hire. GoogleWorkspaceId links to SSO identity*

**OnboardingWorkflow** (75%)
Fields: `id`, `employeeId`, `status`, `startDate`, `targetCompletionDate`, `actualCompletionDate`, `currentStepId`, `createdBy`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
Relations: belongs to Employee · has many Tasks · has many Documents
*Represents entire onboarding process for an employee. Status tracks progress through workflow steps*

**Document** (65%)
Fields: `id`, `employeeId`, `workflowId`, `name`, `type`, `status`, `storageLocation`, `requiresSignature`, `signedAt`, `signedBy`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
Relations: belongs to Employee · belongs to OnboardingWorkflow
*StorageLocation field structure depends on vendor_document_storage decision. Signature tracking fields depend on vendor_esignature selection*

**Task** (80%)
Fields: `id`, `workflowId`, `title`, `description`, `type`, `status`, `assignedTo`, `dueDate`, `completedAt`, `sortOrder`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
Relations: belongs to OnboardingWorkflow · may reference Document
*Generic task entity for checklist items, document completion, training modules, etc. Type field supports extensibility for different task kinds*

**Notification** (75%)
Fields: `id`, `recipientEmail`, `type`, `subject`, `body`, `status`, `sentAt`, `metadata`, `createdAt`
Relations: may reference Employee · may reference Task
*Audit trail for all automated communications sent via Gmail API. Metadata stores template variables and context*

**AuditLog** (60%)
Fields: `id`, `entityType`, `entityId`, `action`, `performedBy`, `changes`, `timestamp`, `ipAddress`
Relations: polymorphic relation to any entity
*Depth and retention requirements depend on compliance_requirements resolution. May need enhancement for HIPAA/SOC2*

### API contracts

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Auth | Confidence |
|--------|----------|---------|------|------------|
| `POST` | `/api/employees` | Create new employee record and initialize onboarding workflow | Google OAuth required, HR admin role | 85% |
| `GET` | `/api/employees/{id}` | Retrieve employee details and onboarding status | Google OAuth required, HR admin or employee themselves | 85% |
| `GET` | `/api/workflows/{id}` | Get detailed workflow state including all tasks and documents | Google OAuth required, HR admin, hiring manager, or assigned employee | 80% |
| `GET` | `/api/workflows/{id}/tasks` | List all tasks for a workflow with filtering and status | Google OAuth required, authorized users for workflow | 80% |
| `POST` | `/api/tasks/{id}/complete` | Mark task as completed and trigger downstream workflow actions | Google OAuth required, task assignee or HR admin | 75% |
| `POST` | `/api/documents` | Upload or generate document for employee workflow | Google OAuth required, HR admin role | 70% |
| `GET` | `/api/documents/{id}` | Retrieve document metadata and download URL | Google OAuth required, document owner or HR admin | 75% |
| `POST` | `/api/documents/{id}/sign` | Initiate e-signature process for document. Implementation blocked pending vendor_esignature selection | Google OAuth required, document assignee | 40% |
| `GET` | `/api/notifications` | Retrieve notification history for debugging and audit purposes | Google OAuth required, HR admin role | 75% |

### Architecture blockers

- **E-signature Integration Layer component design** — blocked by `vendor_esignature at 0.1 confidence - cannot design integration without knowing vendor SDK (DocuSign, HelloSign, PandaDoc, or custom). Different vendors have incompatible integration patterns (REST API vs embedded iframe vs webhook-driven).`
- **Document entity signature tracking fields** — blocked by `vendor_esignature at 0.1 confidence - signature status, provider-specific IDs, and audit trail fields vary significantly by vendor. Cannot define schema until vendor selected.`
- **Document Storage Layer architecture** — blocked by `vendor_document_storage at 0.1 confidence - implementation varies dramatically between Google Drive API (URL references), S3/GCS (object keys), or PostgreSQL BLOB (binary storage). Integration patterns and error handling completely different.`
- **Deployment platform and hosting provider** — blocked by `compliance_requirements at 0.1 confidence AND deployment_model at 0.1 confidence - cannot commit to Vercel, AWS, GCP, or other platform without knowing HIPAA/SOC2 needs. Also blocked by budget_constraints at 0.4 confidence.`
- **Workflow engine complexity and state machine design** — blocked by `workflow_complexity at 0.1 confidence - cannot determine if simple linear state machine sufficient or need full workflow engine like Temporal. Conditional branching, approval chains, and multi-stakeholder workflows require different architectural patterns.`
- **Document generation templating engine** — blocked by `workflow_complexity at 0.1 confidence - template complexity unknown. Simple forms could use pdf-lib, complex documents with conditional sections need Handlebars or React-PDF. Also potentially blocked by vendor_esignature if vendor provides templating.`
- **Audit logging depth and retention policies** — blocked by `compliance_requirements at 0.1 confidence - basic audit logging designed but depth requirements for HIPAA/SOC2/financial regulations unknown. May need dedicated audit database, immutable logs, and specific retention periods.`
- **API webhook endpoints for e-signature callbacks** — blocked by `vendor_esignature at 0.1 confidence - webhook payload structure, authentication method, and retry logic depend on vendor selection. Cannot design endpoints without vendor documentation.`

## Risks

| Severity | Area | Description | Mitigation |
|----------|------|-------------|------------|
| BLOCKING | E-signature Vendor Selection | vendor_esignature remains at 0.1 confidence blocking critical document signing workflows. Budget uncertainty means expensive vendors like DocuSign ($15k-$27k annually for 50 users) may not be affordable. Delayed decision will block development of Document and Task completion flows. | CEO must clarify whether legal e-signatures required or digital acknowledgment sufficient. If legal signatures required, confirm budget can support vendor subscription costs. Consider phased approach: launch with digital acknowledgment, add legal e-signature later. |
| BLOCKING | Compliance Requirements Unknown | compliance_requirements at 0.1 confidence blocks deployment platform selection, document storage architecture, and audit logging depth. Cannot design proper security controls without knowing industry (healthcare/finance require HIPAA/SOC2). | CEO must identify company industry and jurisdiction immediately. This is foundational information that should have 0.9+ confidence. Schedule compliance review call with client. |
| HIGH | Budget Ambiguity | budget_constraints at 0.4 confidence with $20k-$40k range is too vague for architectural decisions. Vendor subscriptions alone could consume entire lower-end budget. Cannot commit to managed hosting (Vercel, Supabase) vs self-hosted infrastructure. | CEO must get explicit budget approval from client including separate line items for: development, vendor subscriptions, hosting/infrastructure, ongoing maintenance. CTO correctly flagged this issue. |
| HIGH | Workflow Complexity Undefined | workflow_complexity at 0.1 confidence makes it impossible to design workflow engine, document templating, or estimate development effort accurately. Unknown whether simple linear workflow or complex branching with approvals needed. | CEO must conduct workflow discovery session with client HR team to map current process. Document types, approval chains, conditional logic, and integration points must be defined before development starts. |
| HIGH | Deployment Model Blocked | CTO correctly blocked deployment_model pending compliance resolution. Vercel is cost-effective ($20/mo) but cannot support HIPAA. AWS/GCP with proper compliance costs $200+/month plus DevOps effort. | Resolve compliance_requirements immediately. If HIPAA/SOC2 required, budget must increase or scope must reduce. Consider compliance-focused platforms like Aptible ($500+/mo) if regulated industry. |
| MEDIUM | Document Storage Strategy Uncertain | vendor_document_storage at 0.1 confidence blocks Document entity design and storage layer architecture. Google Drive API is free but may not meet compliance needs. S3/GCS adds cost and complexity. | Tie to compliance_requirements resolution. If no special compliance, use Google Drive API (already licensed). If regulated, provision compliant cloud storage with encryption and audit logging. |
| MEDIUM | Tech Stack Mismatch for Budget | CTO selected custom Next.js/Node/PostgreSQL stack but CTO challenge mentions low-code/no-code (AppSheet, Retool) may be more cost-effective at $20k budget floor. Custom development could exceed budget before MVP complete. | CEO and CTO must align on build vs buy decision. If budget confirmed below $30k, seriously evaluate low-code platforms. If custom development proceeds, define aggressive MVP scope to fit budget. |
| LOW | Google Workspace API Access | Architecture assumes Google Workspace API access enabled and admin approval for OAuth scopes. Setup delays could block development start. | Verify Google Workspace admin access and API enablement in first client meeting. Obtain OAuth consent and API credentials before development sprint 1. |

## Discovery Questions

*Questions to ask the client before proceeding.*

1. **[CRITICAL]** What is your actual budget range for this project, including any ongoing costs for vendors or hosting?
   *Unblocks: budget_constraints, cto_challenge_budget_constraints, qa_scope_feasibility*
2. **[CRITICAL]** What industry is your company in, and are there any specific legal or compliance requirements we need to consider for data handling and document signatures?
   *Unblocks: compliance_requirements, technical_architecture, vendor_esignature*
3. **[HIGH]** What specific documents do new hires need to sign during onboarding, and do they require legally binding electronic signatures?
   *Unblocks: project_scope, vendor_esignature, workflow_complexity*
4. **[HIGH]** Who are the main users of this system, and do hiring managers or other departments need to approve or access onboarding documents?
   *Unblocks: target_users, workflow_complexity, project_scope*
5. **[MEDIUM]** How many new hires do you onboard annually, and how complex are the workflows for different roles or departments?
   *Unblocks: estimated_timeline, workflow_complexity, team_needs*
6. **[MEDIUM]** Do you need to integrate with any systems beyond Google Workspace, like an ATS or payroll system?
   *Unblocks: project_scope, technical_architecture, workflow_complexity*
